ucs.title: [LLM] Sea Turtle (Teal Kurma) Linux web-shell to nohup SSH tunnel chain on internet-facing servers
ucs.description: Hunts Sea Turtle's hallmark Linux post-exploitation: web-facing application is exploited (T1190), drops a web shell (T1505.003), then spawns a Unix shell that backgrounds an SSH/openssl/socat reverse tunnel via nohup so the implant survives session interrupts — the exact chain documented in PwC and Hunt&Hackett reporting on Marbled Dust/Teal Kurma campaigns against telcos and ccTLD registrars.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("httpd","apache2","nginx","w3wp.exe","java","tomcat","php-fpm","node")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("nohup","sh","bash","dash","ksh","python","python3","perl")) AND (Processes.process="*nohup*" AND (Processes.process="*ssh *" OR Processes.process="* -R *" OR Processes.process="* -L *" OR Processes.process="* -D *" OR Processes.process="*/dev/tcp/*" OR Processes.process="*openssl s_client*" OR Processes.process="*socat *" OR Processes.process="*ncat *")) by host, user, Processes.parent_process_name, Processes.process_name, Processes.process, Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | join type=left host [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_path) as webshell_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as webshell_file from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.php" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.jsp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.jspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*/var/www/*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*/usr/share/nginx/*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*/opt/tomcat/*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*wwwroot*") AND Filesystem.action="created" by host | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`]
ucs.kql:
let webProcs = dynamic(["httpd","apache2","nginx","w3wp.exe","java","tomcat","php-fpm","node"]);
let shellSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (webProcs)
  | where (FileName =~ "nohup" or ProcessCommandLine has "nohup ")
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("ssh ", " -R ", " -L ", " -D ", "/dev/tcp/", "openssl s_client", "socat ", "ncat ", "reverse")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath;
let webshells = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
  | where FileName endswith ".php" or FileName endswith ".jsp" or FileName endswith ".jspx" or FileName endswith ".aspx"
  | where FolderPath has_any ("/var/www", "/usr/share/nginx", "/opt/tomcat", "wwwroot")
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (webProcs)
  | project ShellTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, ShellFile=FileName, ShellPath=FolderPath;
shellSpawn
| join kind=leftouter webshells on DeviceId
| where isnull(ShellTime) or (Timestamp - ShellTime) between (-1h .. 24h)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ShellFile, ShellPath
ucs.rationale: Sea Turtle is a Linux-centric espionage actor (unlike most APTs that lean Windows) and is one of the few groups MITRE explicitly maps to T1564.011 'Ignore Process Interrupts' because their tooling (SnappyTCP, custom SSH-based implants) is launched via nohup to detach from the exploited Apache/Tomcat/Nginx parent. Pairing the nohup+ssh/socat tunnel signal with a recently-created scripted web file u

ucs.title: [LLM] Sea Turtle DNS hijack — corporate webmail/VPN FQDN resolving to attacker-owned ASN for AiTM credential capture
ucs.description: Sea Turtle's signature operation is compromising registrars / DNS providers to repoint a victim's mail, webmail, OWA, VPN or SSO hostnames to attacker infrastructure where they front a clone portal with a freshly-issued Let's Encrypt cert (T1608.003) to harvest credentials (T1557). This UC flags corporate FQDNs whose resolved destination IP/ASN deviates from the historical baseline — the exact lateral signal an org would have seen during the Cisco Talos-documented ccTLD/.gov hijacks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as observed_ips from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="mail.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="webmail.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="owa.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="autodiscover.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="vpn.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="citrix.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="sso.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="adfs.*" OR All_Traffic.dest="portal.*") by All_Traffic.dest _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | eval bucket_day=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d") | eventstats values(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d"),observed_ips,null))) as baseline_ips by dest | where _time>=relative_time(now(),"-7d") | mvexpand observed_ips | where NOT observed_ips IN (baseline_ips) | iplocation observed_ips | lookup asn_lookup ip as observed_ips OUTPUT asn as new_asn | eventstats values(eval(if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-7d"),new_asn,null))) as baseline_asn by dest | where NOT new_asn IN (baseline_asn) | stats min(_time) as firstSeen count as hits dc(host) as distinct_clients values(new_asn) as resolved_asn by dest, observed_ips | where distinct_clients>=3 | convert ctime(firstSeen)
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 60d;
let baselineWindow = ago(lookback) .. ago(7d);
let recentWindow = ago(7d);
let hv_pattern = @"(?i)^(mail|webmail|owa|autodiscover|portal|vpn|citrix|sso|adfs|login|remote|gw)\.";
let corp_domains = dynamic([".contoso.com", ".contoso.local", ".gov.tr"]);  // tune to org / monitored ccTLDs
let baseline = DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp between (baselineWindow)
  | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
  | where isnotempty(RemoteUrl) and RemoteUrl matches regex hv_pattern
  | where RemoteUrl has_any (corp_domains)
  | summarize known_ips=make_set(RemoteIP, 200), known_count=count() by RemoteUrl;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > recentWindow
| where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
| where isnotempty(RemoteUrl) and RemoteUrl matches regex hv_pattern
| where RemoteUrl has_any (corp_domains)
| join kind=inner baseline on RemoteUrl
| where known_count > 50  // only domains we have a real baseline for
| where not(set_has_element(known_ips, RemoteIP))
| summarize firstSeen=min(Timestamp), lastSeen=max(Timestamp), hits=count(), affected_devices=dcount(DeviceId), sample_devices=make_set(DeviceName, 10)
        by RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| where affected_devices >= 3
| join kind=leftouter (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | summarize signins=count(), failed=countif(ErrorCode != 0) by IPAddress
  ) on $left.RemoteIP == $right.IPAddress
| order by firstSeen asc
ucs.rationale: DNS-based provider compromise to redirect victim login portals is the defining tradecraft of Sea Turtle / Marbled Dust — this is essentially the only tracked actor for which large-scale registrar/ccTLD hijack is a documented operational pattern (Talos 2019, NSA/NCSC 2019, Hunt&Hackett 2024). Generic AiTM detections look at proxy domains in URL clicks; this UC instead watches the actor's actual mec
